content.problemStatement:
There are 202020 officers in Chefland who can link the PAN to Aadhar.

NNN applications were received for linking PAN. However, due to an internal conflict, each officer intends to process exactly the **same** number of applications.

Determine the **minimum** number of applications that would remain unprocessed.

Note that NNN can be huge and might not fit in an integer.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single integer NNN, denoting the number of applications.


Output Format
For each test case, output the **minimum** number of applications that will remain unprocessed.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Each officer would process 000 applications and 111 application would remain unprocessed.
Test case 222: Each officer would process 111 application and 35−20⋅1=1535-20\cdot 1=1535−20⋅1=15 applications would remain unprocessed.
Test case 333: Each officer would process 666 applications and 127−20⋅6=7127-20\cdot 6=7127−20⋅6=7 applications would remain unprocessed.
